title: AuthProvider combines client construction and header injection; validates credentials at construction
description: The AuthProvider trait has two methods: build_client() and auth_header(). A future contributor might split these into two traits, or move credential validation into auth_header() making it fallible. The current design is correct: build_client and header injection are always needed together and never separately, so splitting adds interface complexity with no benefit. Validating at construction means auth_header() is infallible. The pre-computed HeaderValue avoids per-request allocation and means syntactically invalid tokens fail loudly at startup. The Arc<dyn AuthProvider> blanket impl (mirroring Box<dyn AuthProvider>) is what makes JmapChatClient Clone while using a trait object — do not remove it.
